title: Module 6 & 7 Internet Languages and Tools
subtitle: Test your knowledge on Server-Side Scripting, Database Connectivity, CRUD, Sessions & Cookies, Dynamic Web Applications, and Web Security.

questions.t: In the client-server model, this is the user's device or application that sends requests and displays results.
In the client-server model, this is a system that receives requests, processes them, and sends back the required data.
Communication between the client and server happens over a network, usually through these two protocols.
This type of programming runs on the server and handles request processing, data management, and core application logic.
This type of programming runs in the user's browser and is responsible for rendering the interface and handling interactions.
Server-side programming languages include JavaScript, Python, Java, and this popular server-side language whose full name is Hypertext Preprocessor.
Client-side programming uses HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, along with frameworks like React, Angular, and this framework.
PHP stands for this.
PHP is a server-side language first released in November 1997, created by this person.
PHP supports small to medium size web solutions and can be embedded into this markup language.
This web application framework was first released in January 2002 by Microsoft and supports large to medium size enterprise applications.
ASP.NET is suitable for enterprise level websites, enterprise CRMs, console applications, and this type of enterprise application abbreviated as ERP.
PHP is suitable for Independent Software Vendors (ISVs), startups, billing/invoicing websites, and small or medium _____ websites.
Database connectivity serves as the bridge between applications and databases, enabling software to communicate with this type of system (abbreviated DBMS).
This standardized API lets applications interact with various DBMS using a common set of functions, bridging an application and a database. Its full name is Open Database Connectivity.
This API enables Java applications to connect to databases and execute SQL queries, and is a core component of the Java platform.
This indirect database connection method uses APIs to facilitate communication between applications and databases without direct connections, offering flexibility and scalability.
These indirect connection solutions act as intermediaries, managing communication and data exchange between applications and databases, offering load balancing and transaction management.
This technique enhances database efficiency by reusing existing connections instead of creating, using, and closing them repeatedly.
These databases use structured query language for managing relational data, where embedded queries can be integrated into application code.
These databases offer flexibility for unstructured data using document, key-value, and graph models, catering to scalability and high performance.
These APIs use HTTP protocols for lightweight, efficient access to web services.
These APIs rely on XML-based messaging with robust security and are suited to enterprise applications.
This security challenge in database connectivity refers to the process of transforming readable data into an unreadable format to block unauthorized access.
This transport layer security protocol (often paired as SSL/TLS) protects data in transit between client and server.
This security process verifies a user's identity, while its counterpart determines what the user is allowed to access.
This security process determines a user's access level after their identity has been verified, limiting users to data relevant to their role.
This security measure adds extra security layers beyond just passwords to verify identity, abbreviated as MFA.
This performance issue in database connectivity refers to the delay in data transmission; high values slow response times.
This performance issue in database connectivity refers to the data capacity of a connection; insufficient amounts cause bottlenecks.
This scaling approach upgrades hardware to handle increased load, while its counterpart distributes data across servers.
This scaling approach distributes data across multiple servers to handle increased load, as opposed to upgrading existing hardware.
This best practice in database connectivity involves examining systems and processes to identify vulnerabilities and keep security protocols current.
This best practice in database connectivity involves well-designed queries that use indexes and avoid unnecessary retrieval to reduce server load.
This best practice distributes workloads across multiple servers to prevent overload, improve response times, and support scalability.
CRUD stands for these four basic database operations: Create, Read, Update, and _____.
This CRUD operation adds new records or data entries into a database and is the initial step in data management.
This CRUD operation retrieves data from a database, either by querying based on criteria or displaying all data.
This CRUD operation modifies existing data to keep it accurate and relevant.
HTTP is described as this, meaning all requests sent are independent — which is why sessions and cookies are needed to store user information.
These let web applications store information in the user's browser, which is retrieved every time the user requests a page from the same web server.
This is a way of storing information about a user on the server-side, used in subsequent requests, and represents a sequence of HTTP request/response transactions associated with the same user.
Sessions let you establish variables such as access rights and _____ settings that apply throughout the user's interaction with the app.
This type of web application displays different content and enables user interaction by changing data in response to user actions or inputs, without reloading the entire page.
Compared to dynamic web applications, this type of web application has content that remains the same regardless of user interaction, requires simpler HTML and CSS coding, and has lower server load.
In dynamic web applications, this e-commerce sector example uses a database driver to manage inventory and user data, with real-time updates to product availability and prices.
In this dynamic web application sector, patient management systems dynamically update and display patient records and test results.
In this dynamic web application sector, platforms adapt content to different learning styles based on student interactions and performance using algorithms.
Web security means employing the latest security protocols and implementing controls to ensure the right person with the right _____ can access the right information at the right time.

This key trend in web security uses technologies that analyze large volumes of data to identify unusual patterns and predict potential attacks before they occur, enabling real-time threat detection and automated response.
questions.a: AI and Machine Learning in Cybersecurity

questions.t: This security model operates on the principle of 'never trust, always verify,' requiring every user, device, and application to be authenticated and authorized before access is granted.
This practice, also known as DevSecOps, integrates security into every stage of the software development lifecycle, identifying and fixing vulnerabilities faster before deployment.
This security concern addresses the rapid growth of connected devices, preventing them from becoming entry points for cyberattacks and protecting the overall network infrastructure.

This web application vulnerability targets the server using malicious code to get it to divulge information, especially problematic when the server stores private data like credit card numbers and passwords.

This web application vulnerability (abbreviated XSS) can damage a company's reputation by placing user information at risk, where a malicious script is injected into a site and activates on each visitor's session.
This web application vulnerability (abbreviated CSRF) forces a victim to perform an unintended action on a web app they're already logged into, because the app trusts the victim's browser.
In a CSRF attack flow, the bank issues this to the victim after login, which the hacker later exploits through a forged request.
In web security, this process confirms the user's identity — answering the question 'Who are you?'
In web security, this process determines what a verified user is allowed to access or do — answering the question 'What permissions do you have?'
This protocol is essential for securing web apps by ensuring communication between client and server is encrypted, preventing attackers from intercepting and reading sensitive information.
Weak authentication such as easy-to-guess or reused passwords leaves apps vulnerable to these attacks, where attackers use automated tools to guess usernames and passwords.
This essential security tool (abbreviated WAF) helps protect web apps from attacks including SQL injection and XSS by filtering out unsafe requests before they reach the server.
Vendors release these fixes to address software vulnerabilities, making it important to keep software updated to prevent attackers from exploiting known weaknesses.
This secure coding practice involves checking user input to ensure it's valid and safe, preventing vulnerabilities like SQL injection, XSS, and command injection.
This security principle states that users, processes, and systems should have only the minimum access necessary to perform their functions.
This attack on user session management involves an attacker stealing a user's session ID, taking control of the session to access sensitive info or perform unauthorized actions.
This attack on user session management involves an attacker setting a user's session ID to a predetermined value, then taking control once the user logs in.
This secure coding practice is essential for detecting and resolving security issues, as errors and exceptions can reveal valuable information to attackers about vulnerabilities.
These can pose a significant security risk if not handled properly, as attackers may upload malicious content that compromises the entire application.
This term refers to the strategic and procedural steps taken to safeguard the privacy, availability, and integrity of sensitive data, often used interchangeably with 'data security.'
This data protection principle ensures users can access and use required data even if it's lost or damaged.
This data protection principle automates the transmission of critical data to offline and online storage.
This data protection principle involves the valuation, cataloging, and protection of information assets from facility outages, application errors, machine failure, and malware attacks.
This is a guideline for how data should be collected or handled based on sensitivity and importance, typically applying to PHI and PII.
PHI stands for this type of information that data privacy guidelines typically apply to.
PII stands for this type of information that includes financial info, medical records, SSNs, names, birthdates, and contact info.
This is the procedure of converting data into a secret code that hides its real meaning.
This is the field of encrypting and decrypting information.
This encryption method uses only one secret key to encode and decipher information and is the oldest and most well-known method.
This more recent encryption method uses two separate but related keys — one private and one public.
This deprecated symmetric key encryption technique requires both sender and receiver to have access to the same private key, and has been superseded by AES.
This encryption method requires three distinct keys and three runs of the DES algorithm, and was a temporary solution once DES was seen as too weak against brute-force attacks.
This cryptosystem, named after its three creators, enables public-key encryption and is commonly used by browsers and VPNs to connect to websites.
This is the standard and most secure type of encryption, using symmetric key encryption on fixed 128-bit blocks of data with key sizes of 128, 192, or 256 bits.
AES uses key sizes of 128, 192, or 256 bits, corresponding to this many rounds of encryption respectively: 10, 12, or _____.
